name: Kabbalah Sefirot
shortDescription: The Sefirot are the ten emanations of God in Kabbalah, representing different aspects of the divine and forming the Tree of Life.
description: The Sefirot (singular: Sefirah) are the ten attributes or emanations in Kabbalah, through which Ein Sof (the Infinite) reveals itself and continuously creates both the physical realm and the chain of higher metaphysical realms. They are often visualized as forming the Tree of Life, a central symbol in Kabbalistic thought. Each Sefirah represents a different aspect of God's being and plays a specific role in the unfolding of creation. The Sefirot are: Keter (Crown), Chokhmah (Wisdom), Binah (Understanding), Chesed (Loving-Kindness), Gevurah (Severity/Judgment), Tiferet (Beauty), Netzach (Victory), Hod (Splendor), Yesod (Foundation), and Malkuth (Kingdom). Understanding the Sefirot is crucial for comprehending Kabbalistic cosmology, theology, and spiritual practice. In the context of Tarot, the Sefirot are often mapped onto the Major Arcana, providing a framework for understanding the cards' deeper meanings and connections to the divine. The Tree of Life serves as a map of consciousness and a guide for spiritual ascent, allowing practitioners to explore the different dimensions of reality and connect with the divine source. The Sefirot's influence extends to various esoteric traditions, including Hermetic Qabalah, where they are integrated with other systems of symbolism and magic.

sources.title: Mysticism, Magic and Kabbalah in Jewish Thought
sources.author: Gershom Scholem
